top_signals.title: Intesa Offers to Buy Monte Paschi in €30.6 Bid
top_signals.summary: Intesa Sanpaolo offered to buy Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena for €30.6 billion ($35.3 billion). The Milan-based bank will offer 1.6 of its own shares and €1 in cash for each Monte Paschi share. The bid comes the day after Banco BPM pitched its own merger with Monte Paschi, considered to be the world’s oldest bank. Bloomberg's Manuel Baigorri reports. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Japanese and South Korean navies hold first joint drills in nine years
top_signals.summary: Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi hailed the resumption of exercises as signaling "a new chapter" in bilateral defense cooperation.
